diagnosis: Current leading hypothesis: Restriction of intramolecular motion (RIM: phenyl-rotor/aryl-alkene torsions) suppressing nonradiative decay in aggregates. Latest evidence leaves this partially unresolved and slightly weakens its discriminating support: repeated torsion-snapshot runs still probed the same dihedral [56,0,1,2] and found essentially no S1 energy/oscillator-strength sensitivity (ΔE~0.0016 eV; Δf~0.0002; lowest state remains bright). The key remaining uncertainty is whether a truly central conjugation-relevant rotor (especially adjacent to the nsnc-containing core) can access a darker/weakly allowed state upon twisting; current evidence cannot answer this because dihedral selection control failed. Best next action is a microscopic no-new-calculation parse step to enumerate/confirm available rotatable dihedrals from existing snapshot/structure artifacts, enabling a targeted follow-up on a different, core-adjacent dihedral.
hypothesis_uncertainty_note: RIM remains structurally plausible (many phenyl rotors; no strong donor–acceptor segregation; bright lowest vertical state at the S0 minimum), but the attempted key discriminant—torsion-enabled access to a dark state—cannot be evaluated because torsion snapshots repeatedly used the same dihedral. Until we verify and scan a core-adjacent conjugation-relevant dihedral, we cannot rule out a specific twist-driven nonradiative pathway (including a weak TICT-like channel) that aggregation would suppress.
capability_assessment: Macro can only provide topology/proxy plausibility and cannot resolve aggregate packing or excited-state relaxation pathways. Microscopic can compute low-cost S0 opt + vertical excitations and run bounded torsion snapshots, but current workflow shows inadequate user-level control/verification over which dihedral is actually being scanned; a parsing/listing step is needed to regain control before further torsion calculations.
stagnation_assessment: Incipient stagnation risk: multiple microscopic rounds repeated the same torsion dihedral, yielding redundant evidence. A no-new-calculation dihedral-enumeration step is the most efficient way to break the loop and restore information gain.
contraction_reason: Contract to a zero-cost parsing/listing step to fix the dihedral-selection failure before spending further compute on additional torsion snapshots.
